title: Crispy Irish Cheese Casserole
description: A fresh, gooey Irish cheese casserole that turns golden and crisp when baked, served warm on a banana leaf for a brunch‑friendly twist. Layered with sharp cheddar and creamy gouda, this dish is kissed by sesame seed oil for a nutty aroma and finished with a crunchy panko crust.
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C and line a baking sheet with parchment.
Rinse the banana leaf, cut to fit the sheet, and soak it for 5 minutes.
Whisk eggs with milk, sesame seed oil, thyme, pepper, and a pinch of salt in a bowl.
Layer shredded gouda over sliced cheddar on the banana leaf, then pour the egg mixture evenly.
Sprinkle panko breadcrumbs over the top and press lightly to adhere.
Place the banana leaf bundle on the baking sheet and bake for 20 minutes until the top is golden brown.
Remove from oven and let rest for 5 minutes before cutting.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve immediately.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days.
